criticalChange_2_2_1: Beacon boosting is now ADDITIVE, not multiplicative, AND unpowered beacons were buffed while powered beacons were nerfed. Aqua-stacking is therefore substantially weaker than every pre-June-2026 guide assumes. All `beaconBias` values for aqua below are inherited from the old multiplicative meta and are FLAGGED FOR RE-DERIVATION — do not trust them.
note: Archetypes are the mechanism for conditional mission priority. Your FIRST mission largely commits the run (only ~3 grey beacons exist). `core` missions identify the archetype; `followups` are ordered tiers — items within a tier are equal-value. This replaces hardcoded if/then chains.

archetypes.name: Flying Chest Engine
archetypes.notes: Self-sustaining pull->chest->boon loop. Hoarder makes Blue Beacons largely redundant, so blue is downranked once online.

archetypes.name: Curse Stacking

sequencing.rule: Bank 6 curses before taking a Blue Beacon (6 x +100% = +600% potency cap, per 2.2.1).
sequencing.why: Taking blue early wastes the entire Equilibrium multiplier.
Madman caps at 8 curses; it is a static snapshot, so it never improves after being taken.

archetypes.buffedIn: 2.2.1 doubled Equilibrium (+50%->+100%, cap +300%->+600%). This archetype is now materially stronger than community guides rate it.
archetypes.notes: Inner Peace belongs here despite sounding contradictory: it halves curse DIFFICULTY while curse COUNT payoffs still fire.

archetypes.name: Beacon Reroll Spam
archetypes.notes: Gourmand + Optimism turns each reroll into a directed search for rainbow/orange. Requires reroll income — Pink Beacon (new in 2.2.0) made this archetype substantially stronger.

archetypes.name: Radiance (2.2.0)
archetypes.notes: NEW in 2.2.0 and under-documented. Lights Out feeds Radiance curses; Radiant Hunter converts them to pulls. Encode with low confidence and flag in UI until validated by real runs.

archetypes.name: Sacrifice Stacking
archetypes.notes: All In converts each sac into +3 rerolls at run end. Sacs have diminishing returns (50%/75%/87.5%) so cap the stack at 3 and pivot to rerolls.

archetypes.name: Token Assisted Speedrun
archetypes.notes: Highest ceiling per the source guide. Chronokinesis is a trap without time surplus, so this archetype needs a strict time floor.

archetypes.name: Endgame Cash-Out
archetypes.notes: Not a run plan — a LATE pivot. Knife Edge pays 7 minus challengesRemaining, so it is worth zero until the run is nearly over. Never offer it as an early pick.

archetypes.name: No Commitment
archetypes.notes: The correct fallback when no archetype has formed. These are stateless and never dead. Prefer them over a speculative archetype core when only 1 grey beacon remains.
